run_summary: Portfolio is +1.7% unrealized across 5 open positions, with BLDR, DECK, and SHAK showing gains. Two positions (TXRH, DCO) remain under water and near their expiration horizons. New flags include a DECK re-flag (already held, now showing +8.3% unrealized), an ALK airline fade on macro momentum, a BLDR re-flag on housing policy catalyst, an AEIS financing-8-K re-entry attempt (blocked by re-entry discipline), and KTOS sector beta. Key decisions: hold all current positions through catalyst windows, skip ALK as macro-driven overshoot, add to BLDR on housing bill tailwind (policy catalyst not yet diffused), and re-evaluate AEIS via watch (confidence bar not met for re-entry).

entries.name: Alaska Air Group
entries.catalyst: Oil below $100 on Trump Iran 'final stages' comments; fuel-cost relief narrative
entries.thesis: ALK's outsized move appears driven entirely by the Iran-talks/oil-down narrative rather than any company-specific catalyst — the only 8-K is a 5.07 annual meeting vote, which is non-event. Move magnitude exceeds SKYW (peer) and the broader IWM by a wide margin on volume only 1.2x average, suggesting short-cover/momentum chasing into a single macro headline that can reverse if Iran talks stall. Fuel tailwind is real but lower-quality balance sheet airlines often overshoot peers on these reflex bids.
entries.what_kills: Confirmed Iran framework agreement with sustained oil decline; ALK-specific positive guidance or route news; sector-wide upgrade cycle from sell-side
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on oil/Iran headline sympathy. Move is 10.05% today on only 1.2x average volume, and 8-K is pure boilerplate (5.07 annual meeting vote, non-event). Thesis is that outsized move (vs. SKYW peer, vs. IWM) on momentum/short-cover into single macro headline that can reverse if Iran talks stall. This is a classic fade setup for sector-beta names without idiosyncratic catalyst. Confidence is only 3, and my track record on OVERDONE conf 3 is poor (see trends: OVERDONE 60% overall but that includes higher-confidence calls). Skip rather than fade outright — if Iran de-escalates tomorrow and ALK rolls over sharply vs. peers, re-flag as more obvious short setup. Taking no position now.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource
entries.catalyst: House passes housing affordability bill (CNBC: 'win for investors'); 8-K with 5.02 director/officer changes around annual meeting
entries.thesis: House approved housing affordability bill today (removed industry-opposed build-to-rent requirements — explicitly framed as 'win for investors'). BLDR is a direct beneficiary as the largest building products supplier. Move is on only 1.02x volume despite a meaningful policy catalyst, and the stock is still negative on a 5-day basis — suggesting the housing-bill news hasn't been fully absorbed by funds yet. The 8-K's 5.02 officer item adds some noise but appears coincident with annual meeting (5.07 alongside), likely board-rotation routine.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 item turns out to be unplanned CFO/CEO departure; Senate kills bill; housing data prints weak
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The bull thesis rests on underdiffusion of a policy catalyst that requires Senate passage—a two-step legislative gate with historically low closure rates—yet sizes the position at 6% exploratory conviction as if the first-step passage guarantees material upside. Bear case: The housing bill passed the House but faces Senate confirmation, a non-trivial legislative hurdle with no stated timeline or signal of Senate support in the flag. The thesis frames this as an 'underdone' opportunity because funds haven't absorbed it, but that absence of fund buying may reflect correct pricing of Senate risk rather than a diffusion mispricing. More concretely: BLDR is down 1.51% over five days despite the stated 'win-for-investors' framing and 7.29% single-day pop, suggesting either the market is skeptical of Senate passage or the move has already played out on day-one momentum. Sizing at 6% exploratory for a two-legged legislative catalyst (House done, Senate TBD) with no stated Senate probability or timeline introduces tail risk that the momentum unwinds when Senate inaction becomes clear. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 re-flag with material NEW catalyst: House passed housing affordability bill TODAY, explicitly framed as 'win for investors' and removing industry-opposed build-to-rent requirements. BLDR is the largest building-products supplier and direct beneficiary. Move on 1.02x volume despite meaningful policy catalyst indicates housing-bill news hasn't yet diffused to fund community. Original entry 3 days ago was on 8-K + mortgage rate spike (already +7.29% unrealized). This new information (bill passage today) meets re-entry/add bar: confidence 3 (policy still needs Senate but House passage is material diffusion opportunity), genuinely new catalyst (bill did not exist 3 days ago). Original position is exploratory UNDERDONE conf 3; ADD inherits that tier. Sizing: 6% exploratory. Current notional ~$666; add ~$600 (~8 shares @ $74.15).)

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis rests on underdiffusion of a policy catalyst that requires Senate passage—a two-step legislative gate with historically low closure rates—yet sizes the position at 6% exploratory conviction as if the first-step passage guarantees material upside.
red_team.critique: The housing bill passed the House but faces Senate confirmation, a non-trivial legislative hurdle with no stated timeline or signal of Senate support in the flag. The thesis frames this as an 'underdone' opportunity because funds haven't absorbed it, but that absence of fund buying may reflect correct pricing of Senate risk rather than a diffusion mispricing. More concretely: BLDR is down 1.51% over five days despite the stated 'win-for-investors' framing and 7.29% single-day pop, suggesting either the market is skeptical of Senate passage or the move has already played out on day-one momentum. Sizing at 6% exploratory for a two-legged legislative catalyst (House done, Senate TBD) with no stated Senate probability or timeline introduces tail risk that the momentum unwinds when Senate inaction becomes clear.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Today's 8-K with financing signature (1.01/2.03/3.02) on top of ongoing semicap sector pressure
entries.thesis: AEIS filed an 8-K today with items 1.01 (material agreement), 2.03 (financial obligation creation), and 3.02 (unregistered equity sale) — this signature is a convertible-note or financing deal, not a guide cut. The stock is being lumped in with the broader semicap selloff (ONTO, ENTG, VECO all down similarly) on the back of the Seagate memory-capex commentary, but the company-specific catalyst is a capital-structure event whose dilution/coupon terms could easily be priced in or even bullish if proceeds fund accretive use. The five-day drawdown is steep relative to sector peers and likely overshoots if the financing terms are reasonable.
entries.what_kills: 8-K reveals deeply dilutive equity issuance at a discount, or proceeds earmarked to cover an operational shortfall; semicap selloff intensifies on further negative AI-capex commentary
entries.reasoning: Re-entry flag. AEIS was closed 3 days ago at -7.95% realized loss (OVERDONE conf 3, sector-beta thesis killed by semicap selloff). New flag re-lists as OVERDONE conf 3 with 1.01/2.03/3.02 financing-8-K signature (convertible/structured financing), arguing that the stock was lumped into sector overshoot and financing terms could be accretive. Two re-entry discipline gates: (1) confidence must be ≥4 for re-entry at conf 3 (it is 3, so execution layer will downgrade to WATCH regardless); (2) information must be genuinely new and unknown at exit. The 8-K itself (financing event) existed at prior exit and was the kill catalyst (sector beta, not idiosyncratic catalyst). New flag re-describes the same 8-K with added thesis flavor about convert premium and use of proceeds — but that flavor is analysis of the SAME event I already knew. No genuinely new information has surfaced (no new guidance, no new contract, no management commentary post-exit). Re-entry bar is NOT met. WATCH rather than BUY, pending: (1) confirmation that financing terms are actually accretive (read exhibit), or (2) new idiosyncratic catalyst unrelated to 5/13 8-K.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
entries.catalyst: Iran geopolitical escalation lifting defense sector
entries.thesis: KTOS's 5/15 8-K is item 5.07 (shareholder vote results) — annual meeting boilerplate, not a catalyst. Today's move is straightforward: ITA defense ETF +1.4%, Trump's Iran posture is escalatory (postponed strike at allies' request, with surrender rhetoric on Truth Social), and KTOS is a high-beta unmanned-systems play within defense. Magnitude is appropriate given its typical sector beta of ~2-3x ITA on geopolitical days.
entries.what_kills: Iran de-escalates suddenly; defense ETF rolls over and KTOS holds bid — would suggest unseen company catalyst worth investigating
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 on defense sector beta (ITA +1.36%, Iran escalation). 8-K is boilerplate (5.07 annual meeting vote, 5/15 filing date). Move is +4.09% today, -4.86% on 5-day basis, on 0.8x volume — pure sector sympathy with appropriate beta. Thesis says 'KTOS is high-beta unmanned-systems play' and moves are 'straightforward ITA beta on geopolitical days.' This is not a discovery flag so much as a sector confirmation. No idiosyncratic catalyst, no underdone/overdone call (classification is RATIONAL). No entry warranted; already holding several defense/industrial names (DCO, BLDR) with better catalysts. Skip.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.

Could be M&A speculation (Qorvo has been a recurring takeout rumor name in RF semis), an analyst upgrade, or sympathy with the semi rally — I cannot distinguish at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If move reverses sharply on no news, indicating it was a squeeze or sympathy froth rather than catalyst-driven

entries.thesis: AVAV (drones) is moving substantially above its sector. Iran conflict ongoing per news flow could be driving drone-specific bid, and Kalshi traders are speculating on Anduril as next US govt stake — possible defense-tech speculation spillover. But no 8-K, no earnings catalyst, can't confirm direction with conviction.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.

The pop with extended 5-day move is consistent with a positive Reg FD item (e.g., capital allocation update or guide reaffirmation). Move magnitude is moderate and proportional to a positive disclosure.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is boilerplate conference deck with no new information — would suggest move is momentum-driven, not catalyst

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.thesis: 7% pop on volume multiple of 0.89x is unusual — moves of this size usually carry volume. Empty catalyst_signals. Could be sympathy with networking/AI infrastructure trade or a thin-volume technical squeeze. Cannot articulate either bull or bear thesis at conf 3+.

Possible drivers: sell-side note, M&A whisper, or AI-FPGA narrative tailwind — but I cannot identify which from available evidence. Neither bull nor bear case clears conf 3.

entries.name: Onto Innovation

entries.thesis: ONTO was down 5% over the prior five sessions and is now recovering on a strong semi-equipment tape. The 8-K items (5.07 vote results, 8.01 other) are routine. Magnitude is in line with semicap peers given SMH +3.8%. No mispricing thesis stronger than 'mean reversion + sector beta' is articulable.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation
entries.catalyst: Defensive sector rotation + technical bounce; 5.07 filing is non-substantive
entries.thesis: RGEN's 8-K is item 5.07 (typically annual meeting voting results), which is administrative and not a catalyst. The move is consistent with XLV +1.1% defensive rotation and a bounce off a recent multi-day decline. No clear mispricing — magnitude is appropriate given sector rotation and recent weakness.
entries.what_kills: 8-K exhibit contains something other than vote results (unlikely for 5.07-only)

entries.name: SkyWest, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; likely sympathy with oil-price-driven airline weakness
entries.thesis: SKYW has continued lower over multiple sessions with no 8-K, no recent or upcoming earnings, and on below-average volume. Most likely driver is jet fuel pressure from XLE/oil bid on Iran tensions and broader risk-off cyclical selling, but with no idiosyncratic signal I can't make a directional call at conf 3+.

XBI is down 2.3% so this is not biotech sympathy; it's idiosyncratic and against the tape. Could be a sell-side upgrade, conference presentation reception, M&A rumor, or guideline-inclusion news that didn't surface in the news feed.
